c. w. Post, president of the Citi-
Gems alliance and known through
out the country for his rabid anti
union and open shop views, has a
new scheme with which he expects
to put labor organizations out of
business and leave the field clear
to employers associations and
trusts. Mr.. Post declares that the
labor unions thrive on publicity,
and he proposes that he and his
kind make arrangements with all
newspapers in the country to cen
sorize everything relating to union
affairs. Post has bigger job on
his hands than the car of Russia,
and a million times less Sower.


At the end of the present year
It will be just a decade since the
fight began for the so called open
shop. and at the forthcoming con-
vention of the American Fedora
ton of Labor. at Toronto, Ont,
some interesting data will be pre-
scented to show the results Of the
